Stand over a kettlebell, hinge and bend the knees to grip the handle, and brace the trunk. Extend the hips and knees to stand tall, then hinge to return the bell between the feet.

This exercise involves holding a kettlebell in one hand and standing on one leg while bending forward to touch the kettlebell to the ground. It targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back while also improving balance and stability.

Stand wider than shoulder width with the toes turned slightly out and the kettlebell between the feet. Brace the trunk, hinge at the hips and bend the knees to grip the bell, then extend the hips and knees to stand before lowering it under control.

Hike the kettlebell between the thighs from a braced hip hinge, then snap the hips forward to float it to about chest height. Let it return into the next hinge without squatting or lifting with the arms.

Stand with most of the weight on the working leg and place the other toes lightly behind for balance. Hold the dumbbells close to the legs, hinge at the hips with a braced spine, then extend the working hip to stand.

Starting position: Get down on all fours. Steps: Push one foot back until fully extended, concentrating on the gluteus muscles. Stay for one second, then return to the initial position. Repeat, alternating feet.

exercise_name: Landmine Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise
movement_definition: Step in the direction named by Landmine Reverse Lunge with Knee Raise, lower through the stance hip and knee, and drive back while maintaining knee alignment.
Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Single-Leg Landmine Romanian Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.
Flex the hips and knees to descend in Landmine Squat with the feet planted, then drive through the feet to stand.
Hold the end of a landmine at chest height, squat while keeping the trunk braced, then stand and press the bar upward and forward. Return it to the chest before the next repetition.
Push off the ground and land on one leg and regain balance before jumping to the other leg.
Standing position: Place the band under your feet in the middle of your foot and grasp it with a hammer grip.

Lay on a bench and put your calves behind the leg holder (better if they are hold on around the lower calves). Hold a grip on the bars to make sure the body is firmly in place. Bend your legs bringing the weight up, go slowly back. During the exercise the body should not move, all work is done by the legs.
From a seated leg-curl machine, flex both knees to pull the pad down and back before returning under control.
Stand in the machine and flex one knee to draw the heel toward the glute while keeping the thigh fixed.
Sit with the knee aligned to the machine pivot and the pad above the ankles. Extend the knees to raise the pad without lifting the hips, then lower it under control.
The leg press is a weight training exercise in which the individual pushes a weight or resistance away from them using their legs.
Lower the hack-squat carriage by flexing the hips and knees, then press through the feet to extend both legs.
Place both feet close together on the leg-press platform. Lower the carriage while the knees track with the toes, then press through the feet to extend the hips and knees without locking abruptly.

Sit on the machine and put your feet on the platform, a bit more than shoulder wide. The feet are turned outwards by a few degrees. Lower the weight so much, that the knees form a right angle. Push immediately the platform up again, without any pause. When in the lower position, the knees point a bit outwards and the movement should be always fluid.

Unrack the bar and set your stance wide, beyond your hips. Push your hips back and sit down to a box that takes you below parallel. Sit completely down, do not touch and go. Then explosively stand up. Stay tight in your upper back and torso throughout the movement.

Starting position: Stand with back straight. Steps: Take a large step forward with your left leg. Bring your pelvis down until you almost touch the floor with your right knee. Bring your pelvis back up. Return to the starting position by stepping back. Repeat, switching legs each time.
Sit with the pelvis and back supported and the outer thighs against the pads, press both knees apart through hip abduction, and return without letting the stack drop.
March in place, lifting one knee toward hip height while the opposite arm swings forward. Keep the trunk tall, place the foot softly, and alternate at the prescribed rhythm.
From standing, squat and place the hands on the floor, jump or step the feet back to a high plank without performing a push-up, return the feet to the squat, then stand or jump and repeat.
The Nordic hamstring curl is one of the best lower-body exercises to build posterior leg strength, improve knee health, and prevent injury.
The barbell is held overhead in a wide-arm snatch grip; however, it is also possible to use a closer grip if balance allows.
Hack Squats but with a 1-2s pause at the bottom of the movement. This makes sure that there is no more elastic energy stored in the muscles.

Set the shoulders against the pendulum-squat pads and place both feet securely on the platform. Bend the hips and knees to a controlled depth while the knees track with the toes, then drive through the feet to return.
Set security pins to about the height of your sticking point Lower the bar, rest on the pins for 2s keeping tension Stand up with maximum force.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes pointed forward, and chest tall. Extend your leg straight out; extend both arms in front of you, at shoulder level. Brace your core and look straight ahead. Slowly squat down. (optional) pause at the bottom. Keep your (free) leg and arms extended for the whole duration.
Perform Pogo Jump as quick stiff-ankle hops, rebounding immediately after each forefoot contact with minimal knee bend.

Start with the bar over midfoot, brace, and pull it from the floor with the spine neutral. Accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, then pull under and receive the bar in a partial squat at the front rack before standing.
Stand with the feet about shoulder-width and place the hands lightly behind the head. Sit the hips down between the feet while the knees track with the toes, then drive through the feet to stand.
Perform a bodyweight squat, then reach both arms overhead at the top while keeping the ribs controlled.
From quadruped, extend one hip to drive the heel backward without arching the lower back, then return.

Lie face-down on a bench with your legs hanging off the edge. Bend your hips so your thighs are vertical, bending your knees at the same time so your shins stay horizontal. Straighten out your legs so they are not bent and your toes are pointing backwards horizontally. Return to the original position, and repeat.

Starting position: Stand straight, feet hip-width apart. Steps: Step backward with one leg so it can bend comfortably to a 90 degree angle. Slowly bend both knees to form 90 degree angles. Return to the starting position. Repeat, alternating legs.
Natural Leg Extension is alternative to Leg Extension machine with no equipment.

A Banded Glute Kickback is an exercise performed on hands and knees, where you anchor a resistance band to your foot and kick your heel back and up, squeezing your glute at the top of the movement, while maintaining a stable core and flat back to target and strengthen the gluteal muscles.

The seated dumbbell calf raise is a targeted exercise for strengthening the lower leg muscles. It follows the same movement pattern as the machine version: you place the weight on your knees, and by extending and flexing your ankles, you move the weight.
Starting Position: Sit on the machine with your knees inward and against the pads. Pull the pin to release the pads. Grab the handles on the sides.b.
Lower through coordinated hip and knee flexion in Shrimp Squat, then press through the feet to stand.
Lie on one side with the pelvis stacked and lift the straight top leg away from the lower leg without rolling the trunk, then lower it under control.
Stand with the feet about hip-width apart. Step laterally into a wider stance, sit the hips back into a controlled squat, stand, and bring the trailing foot in before alternating directions.
Stand on one leg and reach the free foot toward successive clock positions using small controlled single-leg lunges.
Extend one knee against the machine pad until the leg is straight, then lower the load without moving the thigh.
A glute bridge, where you use just one leg at a time.

Stand upright and hold weights in both hands if using loads. Brace your core and lift one leg off the ground. Keep your back straight, hinge at the hips while lowering your torso forward, ensuring you don’t rotate your hips. Lower until you feel a stretch in your standing leg's hamstring, then return to standing position. Repeat on both sides.

Hold a dumbbell and balance on the opposite or same-side stance leg as prescribed. Hinge at the stance hip while the free leg reaches back, keep the pelvis square, then extend the hip to return upright.

Hold a kettlebell securely at the chest or side and step one leg forward. Lower until both knees are comfortably bent while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return and alternate sides.

Sit sideways in the leg-press machine with one foot placed securely on the platform. Bend the working hip and knee through a controlled range, then press the platform away through the whole foot without locking the knee.

Sit on a bench for calf raises and check that the feet are half free and that you can completely stretch the calf muscles down. Pull your calves up, going as far (up) as you can. Make at the highest point a short pause of 1 or 2 seconds and go down.
Brace the hands against the sled with the torso inclined and spine neutral. Drive through alternating steps to push the sled along the prescribed distance while keeping the hips and shoulders level.
Stand with one foot on a slider and no external load. Slide that foot outward while sitting into the opposite hip and keeping its knee aligned with the toes, then pull the sliding foot back to stand.
Stand in a comfortable squat stance with no external load. Descend for three seconds, pause at the bottom for one second, then stand in one second while keeping the knees aligned and trunk braced.
Set the Smith bar across the upper back and take a split stance. Lower through both knees while the front knee tracks over the foot, then drive through the front leg to return.
Set the Smith bar across the upper back and place the feet in a stable stance. Bend the hips and knees to a controlled depth while keeping the knees aligned, then press through the feet to stand.

Olympic lift snatch Pull barbell from ground to overhead lockout using a wide grip and overhead squat. Move bar slowly until cleared knees then explosively extend hips, pull bar up and dive under the bar Catch bar overhead with straight arms Push head forward during lockout.
Set up for a conventional deadlift and accelerate a submaximal bar from the floor while maintaining a braced spine, then return it under control for crisp repetitions.
Descend into a shallow squat, then rapidly extend the hips, knees, and ankles to jump vertically. Land softly with the knees aligned, absorb the impact, and reset before the next repetition.
From standing, squat and place both hands on the floor, jump or step the feet back to a high plank, then return them to the squat and stand. Repeat continuously at the prescribed conditioning pace.
Secure the bar across the upper back, brace the trunk, and bend the hips and knees while the knees track with the toes. Reach a controlled depth, then drive through the feet to stand.
Keep your hips square and still, pulling the cable across your opposing leg using only your inner thigh. Don’t let your torso lean or twist — no side bending to help the rep.

Get onto the calf raises machine, you should able to completely push your calves down. Stand straight, don't make a hollow back and don't bend your legs. Pull yourself up as high as you can. Make a small pause of 1 - 2 seconds and go slowly down.
Stand tall, step one foot out while sweeping both arms overhead, then return the foot and arms to the start. Alternate sides continuously at a controlled conditioning pace.
Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.
Stand with a wide stance and the bar over midfoot, grip inside the knees, and brace the trunk. Push through the floor and extend the hips and knees to stand tall, then return the bar under control.

Stand without an external load, feet wider than shoulder width and toes turned out comfortably. Bend the hips and knees while keeping the knees aligned with the toes, then drive through the feet to stand.
Lie supine with both legs extended. Keep the pelvis still as one leg moves laterally away from the midline, return it under control, and complete the prescribed work on both sides.
Start by doing a front squat At the top position, push the bar above your head (similar to a press) Lower the bar to the shoulders.
Stand up straight Lift the balls of your feet, resting on your heels.
Hinge at the hips with a braced spine in Trap Bar Deadlift, then extend the hips to return to standing.
A combination of squat and deadlift principles with the use of a hex/trap bar, easing stress on the lower back by better centering the weight.
Extend one leg while laying down and start raising your body with one leg touching the ground through the heel.
Get a medicine ball, shoulder width stance, squat, thrust the ball as high as possible against the wall and catch.

Place the back against a wall and slide down until the hips and knees reach a comfortable squat angle. Keep both feet planted and hold the position for the prescribed duration without resting on the thighs.
Load one leg through the step or split stance named by Dumbbell Step-Up, then drive through the stance foot to return.
Press the resistance overhead from shoulder height in Dumbbell Arnold Press while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it.
Hold a band at shoulder height, pull the hands apart by retracting the shoulder blades, and finish with the forearms rotating outward. Reverse the motion under control without shrugging.
Hold a resistance band with the elbows bent to 90 degrees and fixed beside the torso, rotate the forearms outward without arching or turning the trunk, and return slowly.

Stand side-on to a low cable with the handle in the outside hand and the cable passing behind the body. Lead with the elbow to raise the arm laterally to shoulder height, then lower under control without leaning or shrugging.
Bend over slightly while holding two dumbbells. Pull the dumbbells up to your chest, keeping your elbows as high as you can.
Sitting with a Weight Plate, used as wheel, in both hands; raised slightly below eye level.
Sit facing the reverse-fly machine and sweep the arms outward through horizontal abduction while keeping the chest supported.
Stand side-on to a cable set near elbow height, keep the working elbow fixed at 90 degrees beside the torso, and rotate the forearm outward before returning slowly.
Stand side-on to a low cable and hold one handle with the working arm. Raise the arm laterally to about shoulder height without leaning or shrugging, then lower under control.
Stand between crossed cables and hold the opposite handles at shoulder height. Open the arms in a reverse-fly path while retracting the shoulder blades, then return under control.

Cable rear delt fly (single arm): Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set around shoulder height. With a slight bend in the elbow, pull the handle out and back in a wide arc so the upper arm moves away from the body. Keep your torso still and focus on the rear delt doing the work. Use light to moderate weight and controlled reps.

Lie chest-down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms out to the sides in a reverse-fly path, squeeze the shoulder blades gently, then lower under control.
Olympic lift clean and jerk. Combination of clean and jerk in one motion.
Start with a barbell on the floor, clean it to the front rack by extending the hips and turning the elbows under, then press it overhead before lowering under control.
Support the torso with the free arm, let the working arm hang relaxed, and create small circles by gently shifting the body rather than actively lifting the shoulder.

Hold two dumbbells, perform a controlled burpee with the hands on the handles, then jump or step the feet forward and drive both dumbbells overhead in one fluid swing. Lower them safely before the next repetition.

Stand or sit holding dumbbells near the shoulders. Press them upward and slightly outward along a comfortable diagonal until the elbows extend, then lower them along the same path without arching the lower back.
Full-body muscles building exercise. This exercise provides a huge range of benefits in terms of strength & size and is extremely functional.

This exercise involves using dumbbells to perform a bent over face pull, which targets the upper back and shoulders. The movement involves pulling the weights towards the face while keeping the elbows high and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
From a front hold of the DBs in a OHP, press above the head, bring back towards the rear of the shoulders, down, and press back forward.

Hold a dumbbell in each hand. Bend at your hips to make your back almost parallel to the floor. Let your arms hang down. Keep your arms in line with your shoulders. bend and lift your elbows out to the sides until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Slowly return and repeat.

Starting position: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Keep a slight bend in your elbows, shoulders relaxed and shoulder blades pulled back. Execution: Slowly raise the dumbbells forward and slightly to the side, at about a 30–45° angle from your body (between a front raise and a lateral raise).

Face pulls with band – standing horizontal pull. Grab the band at face height, pull elbows high and wide towards your face while squeezing shoulder blades together. Trains upper back, rear shoulders and improves posture.
Set a rope cable near face height and pull it toward the face with the elbows high. Retract the shoulder blades and rotate the forearms back, then return under control without shrugging.
Stand holding a weight plate at its sides with the elbows softly bent. Raise the plate forward to about shoulder height without leaning back, then lower it under control.
Raise the handle in a slight arc with controlled movement. Don’t swing your torso to start the rep.
Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms forward to about shoulder height without leaning back, then lower the weights under control.
Hold a handstand against a wall or with secure support. Bend the elbows to lower the head through a controlled range, then press back to straight arms while maintaining trunk tension.
Use a light barbell, perform explosive lift up starting from underneath knee cap level. Lift/raise explosively using hips, at shoulder level. Tempo: 2111.
Begin with the hips high, sweep the chest forward and low between the hands, then extend the arms as the chest rises. Reverse or lift the hips back to the start while keeping the movement controlled.
Lie chest-down on an incline bench with light dumbbells. Raise the straight or softly bent arms forward and outward into a Y without shrugging, then lower under control.
Sit on a bench with 45° incline Ellbows 45° out Move dumbbells down until upper arms are parallel to shoulders Move dumbbells up, meeting overhead.

Olympic lift jerk Hold bar in front on shoulders like push press Bend and extend knees pushing bar up while diving under the bar Catch bar overhead with straight arms using lunge Move feet parallel while holding bar in lockout.

Sit against a moderately inclined bench with dumbbells overhead. Lower them beside the shoulders, rotate the palms upward as the arms sweep forward to meet in front of the chest, then reverse the circular path back overhead.
Press the free end of a landmine bar upward and forward from shoulder height while keeping the trunk braced.
Stand at the end of a landmine, press the bar forward and upward with one arm while allowing controlled trunk rotation, then return to the starting position.
Stand with a dumbbell in each hand and elbows softly bent. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height without shrugging or leaning, then lower under control.
Sit in the lateral-raise machine with the upper arms against the pads. Raise the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height without shrugging, then lower the pads under control.

Setup: Stand with feet together and grip the bar slightly wider than shoulders. Position: Rest the bar on your upper chest. Press: Brace your core and push the bar straight up until your arms lock out. Finish: Control the bar back down to your chest.

Using a landmine attachment with the barbell running at 90 degrees to either side, hinge slightly at the hips and grip the barbell by the plate sleeve. Raise the barbell to chest height, with your upper arm parallel to the floor at the top of the movement.
Push Up performed from a pike position (optional to have feet elevated).
Set security pins to the height of your lower chin Rest bar on pins keeping tension Raise bar over head.
Lie prone, maintain band tension, and press the arms overhead without shrugging or extending the lower back.
You should have the shoulder line in front of your wrists an perform a push-up, maintaining the shoulder in the same position.

Hold a barbell in the front rack, brace, and make a short vertical dip through the knees and hips. Drive the floor away and use that momentum to press the bar overhead, then return it to the rack under control.
Sit with a slight forward lean and raise the dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height with softly bent elbows, then lower without using momentum.
Seated, bent 45 deg forward. Arms fully stretched out, raise arms up to shoulder height and back down.
Stand side-on to a cable with the working elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm inward across the abdomen without turning the trunk, then return under control.

Sit on a bench, the back rest should be almost vertical. Take two dumbbells and bring them up to shoulder height, the palms and the elbows point during the whole exercise to the front. Press the weights up, at the highest point they come very near but don't touch. Go slowly down and repeat.
Press the machine handles overhead from shoulder height, then lower until the elbows return below the wrists.
Stand side-on to a low cable with its line of pull passing in front of the body. Lead with the elbow to raise the working arm laterally to shoulder height, then lower under control without shrugging.

Lie on the working side with that elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm toward the abdomen against the dumbbell, then return through a controlled range without moving the upper arm.
Lie on the side with the working arm on top, elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked against the torso. Rotate the forearm upward while the upper arm stays still, then lower the dumbbell under control.
Press the dumbbell vertically overhead in a controlled motion until your arm is fully extended, without locking the elbow. Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position at shoulder height.
Press the Smith-machine bar overhead from shoulder height while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it along the guide rails.
Face away from a low pulley with an overhand grip on the straight bar, raise it forward to shoulder height with nearly straight elbows, and lower without swinging.

Place both hands on the floor or parallettes, lean the shoulders forward beyond the wrists, and draw the knees tightly toward the chest. Lift both feet and hold the tucked body supported on straight arms without resting the knees on the elbows.
Starting on back, move to the standing position with dumbbell in one hand. Switch hands between reps.

Stand angled to a high cable with one arm extended. Keeping the elbow nearly straight, sweep the handle diagonally down and across the body toward the opposite hip, then return under control without rotating the torso.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand in front of your body. Keep your palms towards your body. Lift you hands straight up until your hands are under your chin, then lower them. Repeat the exercise.
Stand at the guided-bar machine with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Pull the bar upward along the torso with the elbows leading to a comfortable height, then lower under control.

Stand straight, your feet are shoulder-width apart. Hold the SZ-bar with an overhand grip on your thighs, the arms are stretched. Lift the bar close to the body till your chin. The elbows point out so that at the highest point they form a V. Make here a short pause before going slowly down and repeating the movement.
Press the resistance overhead from shoulder height in Half-Kneeling Dumbbell Press while keeping the trunk braced, then lower it.
From a half-kneeling stance, press the landmine bar upward and forward with one arm without rotating or extending the trunk.
